---
title: 'Covenant of Fatherly Fellowship'
date: '2011-10-06T23:38:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7853718449734105702'
blogger_filename: '/2011/10/covenant-of-fatherly-fellowship.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Mens Meeting'
---

<b>EMIC Morning Men's Meeting @ Hutton Financial 7 AM</b><br /><br /><b>Covenant.</b><br /><br /><b>What is a covenant?</b> Someone said it's an unbreakable contract. We also discussed that there are many types of covenants. There are housing association covenants, marriage covenants, business covenants...<br /><br /><a href="http://1828.mshaffer.com/">Websters 1828 Dictionary</a> defines Covenant as:<br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 32px; font-weight: bold;"><a href="http://1828.mshaffer.com/d/word/covenant" style="color: #465131; text-decoration: underline;" title="[13219] 8 ">covenant</a></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span"></span><br /><div style="color: #465131;"><span style="color: #dad496;"><b>COVENANT</b></span>, n. [L, to come; a coming together; a meeting or agreement of minds.]</div><div style="color: #465131;"></div><br /><br /><br /><dd>1. A <b>mutual consent or agreement of two or more persons</b>, to do or to forbear some act or thing; a contract; stipulation. A covenant is created by deed in writing, sealed and executed; or it may be implied in the contract.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>2. A <b>writing containing the terms of agreement or contract between parties</b>; or the clause of agreement in a deed containing the covenant.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>3.<b> In theology, the covenant of works, is that implied in the commands, prohibitions, and promises of God; the promise of God to man, that mans perfect obedience should entitle him to happiness. This do, and live; that do, and die.</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>The covenant of redemption, is the mutual agreement between the Father and Son, respecting the redemption of sinners by Christ.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>The covenant of grace, is that by which God engages to bestow salvation on man, upon the condition that man shall believe in Christ and yield obedience to the terms of the gospel.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd>4. In church affairs, a solemn agreement between the members of a church, that they will walk together according to the precepts of the gospel, in brotherly affection.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"><b style="color: #dad496; font-weight: bold;">COVENANT</b>, v.i. To enter into a formal agreement; to stipulate; to bind ones self by contract. A covenants with B to convey to him a certain estate. When the terms are expressed ti has for before the thing or price.</div><div style="color: #465131;"></div></dd><dd></dd><dd>They covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver. Matthew 26.<br /><div style="color: #465131;"><b style="color: #dad496; font-weight: bold;">COVENANT</b>, v.t. To grant or promise by covenant.</div></dd><br /><br /><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nlt/leviticus/26-9.html">Leviticus 26:9</a></b><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><span class="versenum" style="font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">9</span>&nbsp;"I will look favorably upon you and multiply your people and fulfill my covenant with you.</span><br /><br /><b>Covenant In Business</b><br />I have a covenant of blessing. <b>That covenant allows me to succeed in business</b> despite what my competition does. If fact if my competition is not a believer I will take over that business and those clients just like the Israelites drove out the sinful inhabitants of the land God promised them. If my&nbsp;competition&nbsp;is a believer than I am able to pray for his success and God will bless us both with more than enough business. In fact the <b>competition of believers will only serve to make us sharper and more cutting edge</b>. The bible says that as iron sharpens iron we sharpen each other. So just like Microsoft and Apple sharpen&nbsp;each other&nbsp;and feed on&nbsp;each others&nbsp;innovations, my business whatever it is, will only be sharpened and enhanced by the presence of&nbsp;believers&nbsp;in my competition.<br /><br /><b>Covenant Promises are&nbsp;Conditional</b><br /><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nlt/deuteronomy/28.html">Deuteronomy 28 </a>and <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nlt/leviticus/passage.aspx?q=leviticus+26:9;leviticus+26:14">Leviticus 26: 9 and 14</a></b> say that <b>Obedience&nbsp;</b>causes&nbsp;<b>Blessing </b>and <b>Disobedience </b>causes <b>Curse</b>.<br /><br /><b>Having conditions; Does the new covenant override/supersede&nbsp;the old covenant?</b><br /><br /><b>We have a better covenant with better promises</b>. The gentiles were told repeatedly by Paul not to enter the old law but embrace the new grace. The Apostles of Jerusalem instructed believers to only focus on four things and not all the issues of the Law. Jesus fulfilled&nbsp;the Law. God has placed his law in our hearts now. Therefore the law is still being obeyed, but it's a natural outgrowth of the Love in us, not a forced ritual from the outside.<br /><br />Because of the new birth we get stuff because he loves us as Father. Under the New Covenant we <b>do not DO to GET, we DO from LOVE.&nbsp;</b><br /><br />Who is the New Covenant with? It is between God the Father and Jesus Christ. We enter through Jesus as&nbsp;substitutes, literally as him.&nbsp;Separate&nbsp;from Jesus we have no covenant. We live in his stead on earth and reap his reward.<br /><br /><b>Do VS&nbsp;Receive</b><br />As men, we are very production oriented. In sales we have a do, do, do mentality. We are told over and over must make/receive X amount of calls in order to get certain results. But in Christ we must come to terms with the fact that we were blessed before we DID anything. We cannot carry that do mentality over into our relationship with God. In fact, the do mentality may hinder our sales at work too if we don't first allow the grace of God to lead us in our work. In Christ we don't have to knock on 100 doors before lunch, we have to pray and knock on the doors he tells us to. Then we'll get better results for less&nbsp;superfluous&nbsp;effort.<br /><br />Joshua 1 shows us to meditate on the word and do the word to get results. Genesis 1 shows us that God blessed mankind before man ever had a chance to speak a word. The very first words spoken between God and man were not man speaking to God but God speaking to man. God blessed man before man said or did anything. God is still blessing us before we do anything.<br /><br /><b>God is our source.</b><br />There was a man from,<a href="http://www.oru.edu/academics/college_of_theology_and_missions/graduate_program/thomson_mathew.php"> <b>ORU Dr Thomson Matthew</b></a>, that was speaking on a video for the ministry of helps team and he said that we must come to think of our work paycheck as a bonus not as our main source. We are cared for as kids by our Father in Heaven. We use our "Paycheck" as seed, it is our bonus not our income. God is our income and he can provide a million dollars a billion ways.<br /><br /><b>Fellowship not Checklist</b><br />Good fathers want fellowship with their sons. This is especially true of the father-son relationship as they are both adults. We must remember that our heavenly Father wants fellowship with us. We must not treat prayer time as the part of our checklist saying, "OK... prayed for ten minutes... what's next?" We must spend time fellowshipping with the Father and having a TWO way conversation with him. Only then will we be prepared for our day and the trials that lie ahead.<br /><br /><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nlt/jeremiah/29-11.html">Jeremiah 29:11</a> reminds us that His plans for us are GOOD plans. Not plans to harm. We must not be scared of his will. His Covenant is here for us. We are in Covenant through Him. Put that Covenant to work, trust Him to meet your needs. No matter what life throws at us, we can overcome through the Blood of the Lamb and the Word of OUR Testimony. We put it to work by putting His Word in Our Mouths.<br /><br /><b>Fellowship Defined:</b><br /><b><span class="Apple-style-span"></span></b><br /><h1 style="font-weight: normal;">  <b><a href="http://1828.mshaffer.com/d/word/fellowship" style="color: #465131; text-decoration: underline;" title="[21659] 10 ">fellowship</a></b></h1><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"><b><b style="color: #dad496; font-weight: bold;">FEL'LOWSHIP,</b>&nbsp;n.</b></div><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><br /><br /><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">1. </span>Companionship<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">; society; consort; mutual association of persons on equal and friendly terms; familiar intercourse.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd style="font-weight: normal;"><b>Have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness. Eph. 5.</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div><b></b></dd><dd style="font-weight: normal;"><b>Men are made for society and mutual fellowship.</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">2. </span>Association<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">; confederacy; combination.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd style="font-weight: normal;"><b>Most of the other christian princes were drawn into the fellowship of that war. [Unusual.]</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">3. </span>Partnership<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">; joint interest; as fellowship in pain.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">4. </span>Company<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">; a state of being together.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd style="font-weight: normal;"><b>The great contention of the sea and skies parted our fellowship.</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">5. </span>Frequency <span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">of intercourse.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd style="font-weight: normal;"><b>In a great town friends are scattered, so that there is not that fellowship which is in less neighborhoods.</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">6. </span>Fitness and fondness for festive entertainments<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">; with good prefixed.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd style="font-weight: normal;"><b>He had by his good fellowship - made himself popular, with all the officers of the army.</b><br /><div style="color: #465131;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">7. </span>Communion<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">; intimate familiarity. 1John 1.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">8. In arithmetic, the rule of proportions,</span> by which the accounts of partners in business are adjusted<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">, so that each partner may have a share of gain or sustain a share of loss, in proportion to his part of the stock.</span></b><br /><div style="color: #465131; font-weight: normal;"></div><b></b></dd><dd><b><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">9.</span> An establishment in colleges<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-weight: normal;">, for the maintenance of a fellow.</span></b></dd><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b>Good reminder:</b><br /><b>Pray for your parents and your relationships with your kids. See them how God sees them and ask what He wants for them.</b><br /><br /><br /><b>Darrell G. Wolfe</b>
